hypothesis: Simple baseline without manual feature engineering.
More randomized trees can reduce variance versus RandomForest.
Averaging linear, bagging and boosting probabilities can improve stability.
Bagging over nonlinear trees captures interactions and is robust to outliers.
Dimensionality reduction may keep signal while reducing noisy sparse features.
Credit ratios and external-score aggregates improve a linear model.
